---
title: "02 Overfitting & Cross-Validation"
---

# 🎲 Random

- [landmark.am](https://landmark.am/)

![image.png](../00_random_image/02_ancient_churches_Armenian.jpg)


# 📚 Նյութը

- [📺 [06] Մոդելի գնահատում. overfitting ու cross validation](https://youtu.be/1j_xYLQrUAg), [🎞️ Սլայդեր](04_overfitting_cross_validation.pdf), [📝 Նշումներով](04_overfitting_cross_validation_notes.pdf)

📝 **Թեմայի վերաբերյալ հարցաշար (Google Form):** TBD

***

# 🏡 Տնային

## Project — Predicting apartment rent, *done right* 🧀🧀🧀

Continue HW2 on the same `House_Rent_Dataset.csv` (target `Rent`): last time a plain linear regression, now an honest, tuned model using this chapter's tools.

**Setup.** Data: `../01_regression__main_concepts/data/House_Rent_Dataset.csv` — the same file as HW2, so **reuse your HW2 preprocessing code** (drop `Posted On` / `Area Locality` / `Point of Contact`, one-hot the categoricals, scale inside a `Pipeline`). Deliver one reproducible notebook, seed `509`, ending with a 3–5 sentence conclusion.

**Tasks.**

1. Split off a test set, use k-fold CV on the rest, and touch the test set only at the end.
2. Add `PolynomialFeatures` of rising degree and see whether it overfits (train vs validation error).
3. Regularize with Ridge, Lasso and ElasticNet; look at the coefficient paths and what Lasso zeroes out.
4. Tune the penalty with at least two of `GridSearchCV`, `RandomizedSearchCV`, Optuna.
5. Pick and justify a metric — `Rent` is very skewed, so compare RMSE vs MAE vs R² (and maybe a `log(Rent)` target).
6. Report the final test score once, with a short conclusion.

**Bonus:** `log(Rent)` target via `TransformedTargetRegressor`, quantile regression for a P10–P90 range, per-city error analysis, or parsing `Floor`.


<a href="http://s01.flagcounter.com/more/1oO"><img src="https://s01.flagcounter.com/count2/1oO/bg_FFFFFF/txt_000000/border_CCCCCC/columns_2/maxflags_10/viewers_0/labels_0/pageviews_1/flags_0/percent_0/" alt="Flag Counter"></a>
